summary.question: Is the current MVP valuable enough to use beyond internal development?

notes: Text evidence is useful for internal channel diligence.
Text metrics remain direction-only and are not approved for external claims.

channel_observations.mvp_signal: Has a modest positive internal text signal and one internally interesting RR draft, but sample size and provider/operator gates block customer use.
Crypto-only routing is cleaner than mixed MOEX/crypto sources, but the media setup lacks target/position fields.
Largest sample and richest media candidates, but performance is near-even and several strong-looking media rows are post-factum.

insights.title: The current MVP already creates internal diligence value.
insights.detail: It can gather public evidence, normalize directional claims, separate confirmed and contradicted outcomes, and prevent unsafe promotion of media/chart claims.

insights.title: The strongest near-term wedge is analyst-grade source diligence, not a public signal leaderboard.
insights.detail: The system is strongest when it explains evidence quality, exclusions, blockers, and review burden. It is not yet strong enough to sell customer-facing performance metrics.

insights.title: Automation is working as a risk reducer.
insights.detail: The auto-validation stack rejected post-factum candidates and routed ambiguous rows to human review instead of creating false customer-facing wins.

insights.title: The main bottleneck is accepted evidence, not another dashboard surface.
insights.detail: The project has 0 operator-accepted media claims, 0 recomputed accepted outcomes, 0 buyer-demo-safe rows, and 0 customer-facing rows.

insights.title: Buyer value is still unproven.
insights.detail: The product has not yet validated whether target buyers will pay for diligence reports, trust public-source evidence, or change their workflow based on these artifacts.

startup_pressure_test.interpretation: The product has a credible technical wedge and fast validation path, but buyer urgency and willingness to pay remain assumptions.

go_no_go.next_validation_goal: Prove whether a buyer wants an analyst-grade diligence report before investing in more customer-facing automation.

next_experiments.title: Five buyer discovery calls around source diligence pain
next_experiments.success_signal: At least 2 buyers describe a current manual or paid workflow for vetting signal sources and ask to see a bounded diligence report.
next_experiments.failure_signal: Buyers treat this as curiosity, free content, or a dashboard feature with no budget.

next_experiments.title: One operator-accepted media row recomputed end to end
next_experiments.success_signal: A single candidate has accepted timing, asset/proxy, direction, entry, stop, target, horizon, recompute provenance, and policy-gate status.
next_experiments.failure_signal: The team cannot obtain enough evidence to validate even one row without speculative interpretation.

next_experiments.title: Concierge internal diligence report for one source
next_experiments.success_signal: A target buyer says the report would affect source selection, risk review, or paid-vendor diligence.
next_experiments.failure_signal: The buyer only wants raw hit rate, trading advice, or unsupported rankings.

kill_criteria: No buyer confirms an existing painful workflow for source diligence.
Public-source evidence is considered insufficient for any purchasing or operational decision.
Operator-accepted media validation cannot produce at least one recomputed row.
Buyers only want forward-looking trade recommendations, which the project must not provide.
